task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 12 How to install the Magnetic Media Kit

abstract.p: The Magnetic Media Kit supports magnetic media from roll 1.
For certain magnetic print media the guide plates in the media drawer need to be replaced with a stainless steel version.
This document describes how to replace 4 guide plates, springs of the feed roller, plastic guidance-plates on the cover, and new plastic stops.
The Magnetic Media Kit is only used for media roll 1.

note.p: As the MLO springs for media from roll 1 are changed with this kit, this might have an effect on regular media. If it does: use roll position2 for this media.

li.p: Please read A5010, Guidelines for working with the Magnetic Media Kit on graphiPLAZA.
Go to 21-6-040 Go to "cold" SDS [Special Tests] and disconnect the Service jumper.
Open the media drawer.
Remove the rolls with media.
Screwdriver Torx T20
Screwdriver Torx T10
Small Torx T20 key in a rattle
Needle nose pliers
Flat head screwdriver.
Magnetic Media Kit
4 stainless steel guide plates
2 springs (for the rollers on guide plate 2)

note.p: These springs are designed for use with magnetic media.
However, they may not be suitable for handling other types of regular media, which is why you should not install them by default.
We recommend keeping the unused springs, along with any other spare parts that may not be initially needed for your printer."
On some versions of the guide plate, you will find a reflective sticker designed to reflect light for the Media loading sensor
This sticker also needs to be transferred to the new Non-magnetic guide plate.
If this is not possible please contact CPP service & support LFG via the normal channels. (This sticker will be added as soon as possible to the kit)

li.p: 6 Plastic stops
6 Plastic Guide Plates
4 Anti-static brushes
3 Cable, Holder 10,2x16,5 mm
14 Cable, Holder 5,2x11 mm

section.title: Location of the guide plates in the media drawer of the printer

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks
Remove the cover (8 screws) at the left and right hand side.

section.title: Removal and install of guide plate 1 and 2

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks

li.p: Remove the lower screw on both side of guide plate 1.
Loosen the upper screw on both side of guide plate 1.
Unhook the guide plate at the top
Lift and remove the guide plate.

entry.p: Remove the small and large roller from the guide plate assembly.
They need to be installed on the new guide plate.
Optionally, replace the 2 stronger springs when the customer will mainly use Magnetic-Media, which needs a higher pinch force.

note.p: The delivered springs in de magnetic media kit may cause issues with regular media.

entry.p: Remove of the rear cover (to get access to the rear of guide plate 2):

li.p: On the right loosen the 2 screws.
On the left loosen the nut (13mm) and screw.
Lift and remove the rear cover.
Disconnect the connector of the motor.
Unhook the fixed connector from guide plate 2.

entry.p: Remove the cable from all cable guide clips on the guide plate.

note.p: Do not forget the cable clips under the motor.

li.p: Remove the 3 screws.
Place the removed components inside the media drawer.

entry.p: Remove 2 x 3 screws of guide plate 2
You will need a special torx key for this. (Torx key in a rattle)

note.p: Take care of the screws, they frequently fall into the media drawer.

entry.p: Disconnect the large roller from the axis "A" and remove guide plate 2.
Move the large roller from the old guide plate (right hand) into the new one (at the left hand).

li.p: Remove both E-rings
Remove the bearings.
Remove the roller

entry.p: Revers order on the new guide plate.
Remove the media cutter counter blade from the old guide plate and install it on the new one.
Install the plastic stops on both sides.
2 on either sides on the closest position to the bend.
Install the new cable holders to the new guide plate. Take care because there are different sizes.
The first 3 on the edge are the big cable-holders.
The rest are the small cable-holder.
Install the new guide plate in the drawer and reconnect the axes to the roller.
When you seat the guide plate make sure the stops go into the correct holes in the mounting. (on both sides)
Tighten the guide plate on both sides with the 3 screws.

note.p: Take care of the screws, they frequently fall in the media drawer.

li.p: Reseat and reconnect the motor connection.
Guide the cables through the cable guides
Mount the sensors back on the guide plate.
Guide the cable between the speed sensor and the guide plate as shown.

entry.p: Re-install the rear plate previously removed.

li.p: Tighten the 2 screws at the right hand side
Tighten the nut and screw at the left hand side.

entry.p: The rollers removed from the old guide plate 1 need to be mounted on the new guide plate.
The large roller needs to be mounted with the new springs attached.
On some versions of the guide plate, you will find a reflective sticker designed to reflect light for the Media loading sensor .
If this sticker is not in the machine, be sure that MOD03 has been installed.
This sticker also needs to be transferred to the new Non-magnetic guide plate.
If this is not possible please contact CPP service & support LFG via the normal channels. (This sticker will be added as soon as possible to the kit)

note.p: These springs are designed for use with magnetic media.
However, they may not be suitable for handling other types of regular media, which is why you should not install them by default.
We recommend keeping the unused springs, along with any other spare parts that may not be initially needed for your printer."

li.p: Mount the new guide plate in the drawer.
Hook in the upper screws.
Insert the lower screws on both sides and then tighten all 4 screws.

fig.title: Install and tighten right hand side
Install and tighten left hand side

entry.p: Make sure all guide plates are installed correctly and that the paper path closes correctly.

section.title: Removal and install of guide plate 3

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks
Prepare the new guide plate 3:

li.p: Clean the side of the new guide plate with alcohol to remove any grease.
Remove the sticky tape from the anti static brushes (2x) and mount them.
Align with the holes in the middle of the guide plate.
Install a stop on both ends of guide plate 3.

entry.p: Open the media cover.
Remove both screws and remove the old guide plate.

li.p: Install the new guide plate 3.
Lock it wit 2 screws.

entry.p: Close the cover.

section.title: Removal and install of guide plate 4

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks

li.p: Clean the side of the new guide plate 4 with alcohol to remove any grease.
Remove the sticky tape from the anti-static brushes (2x).
Start applying the anti-static-brush about 7 to 8 cm from the edge of the plate and align them with the edge of the guide-plate 4. Start applying the 2nd brush right after the first one.

fig.title: Distance from the edge of the guide-plate

li.p: Remove 1 screw each on both sides of Plate Guard Large on the Winder-side.
Remove Plate Guard Large from the engine.
Remove 1 screw each on both sides of the Plate-Guide-Assy.
Remove Plate-Guide-Assy from the engine.
Install new Guide-Plate 4 in the same way as Plate-Guide-Assy was deinstalled in step 4
Install Plate Guard Large in the same way as it was taken out in step 2

section.title: How to install the Magnetic Media Upgrade Kit

p.xref: 12 How to install the Magnetic Media Upgrade Kit

section.title: Installation of the Plastic Guide Plates (6x)

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks

li.p: Mount the 6 Plastic Guide Plates on the Back Cover of the Engine.
Mount them so, that the magnet strips fit right between the bumps of the cover
Start from the middle and install 1 Plastic Guide Plate 120 mm to each side
Install the rest of the plates at a distance of about 240 mm from each other.
Mount the Guide Plates flush with the bottom edge of the back-cover.
Watch specifically that all plastic strips are placed high enough to fit under the plastic-flap.

fig.title: Aligning Guide Plates with bottom edge
Aligning Guide Plates with top edge

li.p: The plates are adjustable to fit every media-width. So if a narrower media is run, the customer can adjust the plates to be right at both ends of the media

fig.title: Possible alignment for more narrow media

section.title: Final check (Make sure the printer is functioning correctly from media roll 1)

entry.p: Step
Action
Remarks
Load media in roll 1 position.
Measure the media thickness of the installed media.
Enter the media thickness in µm in the corresponding parameter.
MediaThicknessServiceTestPaper: 25-1-006 Media thickness of Service tests - Paper media [um] [Parameters]
MediaThicknessServiceTestVinyl: 25-1-004 Media thickness of Service tests - Vinyl media [µm] [Parameters]
MediaThicknessServiceTestFilm: 25-1-005 Media thickness of Service tests - Film media [um] [Parameters]
For details see : Task [A] Set the media thickness for the calibration media [Tasks]
See 12 How to measure the media thickness
Load the media for the media calibration on roll 1:

li.p: minimum width 50 inch (1270 mm)
white and smooth surface
the media should not be too thin
the media should also be usable in loose winding
the media should fall in one of the media categories "film", "paper", "vinyl".
Example: MPI 2000 (preferred)

note.p: When you load the media, you have to enter the media width by hand.

entry.p: Close the media drawer.
Set the output configuration to Wind the output = NO.
If needed: Calibrate the media.
Print the Demo Print from the operator panel.
Do SDS test 19-1-015 and set Magnetic media hardware installed to "1"
